helpful: Provisioned questions-presented/petition/BIO texts — the core of the analysis
Statpack relist-count and per-Term paid/IFP cuts — the main quantitative anchors
CourtListener dockets endpoint for companion-petition status (hold-cluster signal)
gaps: fedcourts query returned zero priors for both citation-overlap and topic filters on scotus/2020s — a semantic or QP-text similarity search over resolved cert petitions would have been far more useful
No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR) petitions — the single strongest observable signal here has no in-corpus base rate; a CFR flag per docket would enable it
The statpack salience-band table referenced in the predict prompt is not present in the committed statpack.md/json
SCOTUS dockets are largely absent from the CourtListener search index; only the REST dockets endpoint (exact docket_number) worked for companion-case lookups
notes: One corpus query cost 418 ranged GETs / ~110 MB and returned nothing; cheap-but-empty retrieval suggests the citation index has little scotus-2020s coverage. The docket JSON's own post-conference silence (no entries after Mar 30 in a July 10 snapshot) was the most informative single fact — snapshot recency metadata like sJsonCreationDate is worth surfacing explicitly to predictors.
